actions.text: ## Key Decisions and Agreements
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Vrijen to implement emotional benchmarking system** - Alex recommended creating a baseline measurement of Vrijen's current post-retreat mental state to detect future burnout earlier. Matters because burnout descent is gradual ("frog in boiling water") and hard to notice without reference points.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Focus GTM strategy on hiring managers, not just founders** - Reconfirmed strategic direction to differentiate approach by stakeholder type (founders vs. early talent vs. hiring managers). Matters because hiring managers have different pain points and decision criteria than founders.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Explore "unsexy company" tech talent placement niche** - Discussion surfaced potential differentiation by targeting non-tech companies (like Coca-Cola) seeking tech talent who are "bottom of pile" for candidates. Matters because this could be less crowded market with stronger value prop.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Test "baseball cards" / candidate package gimmick** - Agreed to explore creating physical deck of 52 top candidates as awareness-raising stunt and sales tool. Matters because it creates tangible way to demonstrate talent pool quality and community strength.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Implement FOMO mechanics for hiring manager engagement** - Inspired by StubHub, show hiring managers "recently placed" candidates to create urgency and validate subscription model. Matters because creates logical reason for ongoing subscription vs. one-time placement fee.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: ## Strategic Context
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Vrijen returned from week-long wellness retreat in Arizona following burnout period characterized by worst-ever productivity despite maximum concern about productivity. He's implementing "task bankruptcy" concept - acknowledging some follow-ups won't happen and moving forward with clean slate. This emotional reset is critical timing for strategic planning.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Current business position: Future of Higher Education group committed to pilot (couple hundred users), pursuing HBS and Zooglers communities. Pain point is insufficient traction with companies/startups on hiring side. Alex positioned conversation around surfacing right information to hiring managers to help them "see candidate in the role" - the crucial visualization moment that drives hiring decisions beyond resume screening.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Competitive landscape discussion revealed automation tools (Zapier, N8N) largely fail despite popularity - "conspiracy theory that no automations work, everyone just pretending." Vrijen positioning Zo (the AI tool he uses) as alternative with natural language workflows vs. diagrammatic approach.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Key tension emerging: Should Careerspan focus on (A) founders/Series A and below with loose/opaque decision-making, (B) HR at larger companies with established budgets, or (C) non-tech companies seeking tech talent? Each requires different go-to-market motion.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window
